description_long: This object is a compact, box-shaped electronic device with a weathered, industrial appearance, likely intended for hazardous or high-security environments. The front face features a large red button with an atomic symbol, a yellow warning label, and a metallic wind-up key on the side, suggesting manual activation or reset. The top has a coiled metal antenna or wire, possibly for signal transmission or remote activation. The right side displays two large, ribbed indicator lights—one yellow and one orange—while the back has a circular black panel and a radiation warning symbol, reinforcing its association with dangerous or high-energy operations. The left side is marked with a high-voltage warning triangle, and the entire device is constructed from metal with visible bolts and signs of wear. Its design suggests it could be used as a control or emergency shutdown device in urban or industrial settings.
description: A rugged, boxy electronic device with a large red atomic button, warning labels, indicator lights, and a coiled antenna, designed for hazardous or industrial environments.
description_view_0: Front view shows the red atomic button, warning label, and wind-up key prominently.
description_view_1: Right side view highlights the two ribbed indicator lights (yellow and orange) and the coiled antenna.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a black circular panel, radiation warning symbol, and the back of the wind-up key.
description_view_3: Left side view features the high-voltage warning triangle and the side profile of the red button and wind-up key.
